label: cli-prompt-reply-and-plan-output
source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iqNzfK4_meQ ("Using OpenAI Codex CLI with GPT-5-Codex", official OpenAI YouTube channel) @ 02:45-03:05
interaction: Real macOS Terminal recording (screen-recorded insert within the vendor video, intercut with talking-head cutaways -- not a continuous single take). Sequence: (1) types the command "codex" character-by-character at a bare zsh prompt in a `gpt-5-coding-examples` project dir; (2) cuts to a `codex resume` session picker ("Resume a previous session" list of prior sessions with relative timestamps and first-line prompt text, footer hints 'Enter to resume / Esc to start new / Ctrl+C to quit / <-/a prev, ->/d next'); (3) talking-head cutaway; (4) cuts to a terminal already showing a long, fully-rendered agent plan response (multi-section markdown-style plan for a WebRTC multiplayer game -- ICE_SERVERS const, numbered implementation steps, headers) filling the scroll-back, with the user typing a natural-language reply into the composer character-by-character ("Awesome! Let's" -> "Awesome! Let's implement the pl..."), footer showing token/context usage ('25.1K tokens used, 96% context left'); (5) cuts back to talking-head.
note: IMPORTANT CALIBRATION CAVEAT: this clip does NOT show the agent's response streaming in token-by-token -- every terminal shot is a jump cut to an already-rendered state (the plan output in step 4 is fully present the instant the shot begins; there is no visible in-progress/partial-render frame in this footage). What it DOES calibrate: (a) per-character typing cadence and caret idiom in the Codex CLI composer (both for the launch command and for a composer reply), (b) exact visual layout/density/wrapping of a completed multi-section agent plan response in the transcript pane, (c) the `codex resume` session-picker UI (list rows, relative timestamps, footer key hints), (d) footer chrome during an active session (token count, context-remaining percentage). For the actual streamed-response reveal (text appearing progressively, any streaming cursor/spinner idiom), no matching footage was found in this source within budget -- the motion judge should fall back to product knowledge/first-principles pacing (typical LLM token-by-token or chunk-by-chunk reveal) for that specific beat, or a follow-up acquisition pass should search a longer-form Codex CLI screencast (not a produced marketing video, which favors jump cuts over real-time capture) for an unedited streaming segment.
frameExtraction: ffmpeg -i clip.mp4 -vf fps=2 frames/f-%03d.png (40 frames over 20s)
appVersion: Codex CLI as shown in vendor video (unpinned; not cross-referenced against a local install for this pass)
